behavior_summary: This script drives a simple 'Torax' item/NPC that visually alternates between "wounded" and "healed" frames, reports its current visible state when examined by the player, responds to hits with a sound, and schedules periodic/randomized toggles while a player is nearby.

U8P_WOUNDTOR::look: when the player 'looks' at the object, the script reads Item::getFrame(this) and displays a short bark: frames 0 or 1 produce "Wounded Torax", other frames produce "Healed Torax"; the process then suspends.
U8P_WOUNDTOR::enterFastArea: begins the object's timed/toggle behavior by calling U8P_WOUNDTOR::func013A when an actor enters the trigger/fast-update region.
U8P_WOUNDTOR::leaveFastArea: performs cleanup by calling FREE::resetRef(0x00F0h, this) to unregister or stop scheduled behavior when the actor leaves the area.
U8P_WOUNDTOR::gotHit: plays a hit sound effect (playSFX) when the object is damaged.

U8P_WOUNDTOR::func013A: inspects Item::getFrame(this) and toggles frames (0 <-> 1). When flipping from frame 1 -> 0 it plays a sound, computes a randomized delay (e.g., 0x23 + urandom(0x14)), and spawns a deferred call to FREE::func1C19; this routine contains suspend points and appears to drive the object's periodic/randomized appearance changes.
notable_control_flow: Look handler branches on Item::getFrame(this): frames 0 or 1 -> wounded bark; else -> healed bark (simple two-way classification).

U8P_WOUNDTOR::enterFastArea immediately calls U8P_WOUNDTOR::func013A to start or advance the toggle schedule; U8P_WOUNDTOR::leaveFastArea cancels scheduled work via FREE::resetRef, forming a start/stop pair tied to player proximity.

U8P_WOUNDTOR::func013A contains a two-branch toggle (if frame==0 set 1; else if frame==1 set 0 + play SFX), followed by randomized spawn scheduling — combining immediate state change with deferred continuation.
U8P_WOUNDTOR::gotHit is a short, non-branching process that just triggers a sound effect for feedback.
open_questions: Which external routine exactly handles the spawned follow-up (FREE::func1C19) and what state or cleanup it performs after the delay? (inferred only from spawn call).
Whether frames other than 0 and 1 represent additional visible states or are treated uniformly as 'healed' by design (inference: any non-0/1 => 'Healed Torax').
Exact semantics of the resetRef reference id 0x00F0h (is it the pid returned by spawn or a different registration token?).
Are the process parameters passed into U8P_WOUNDTOR::gotHit (uword, word) used elsewhere in the full script/engine for damage logic, or are they unused here?

main_function.name: U8P_WOUNDTOR::look
main_function.purpose: Report the visible condition of the Torax object to the player when inspected: if the item's animation/frame is 0 or 1 the object appears wounded, otherwise it appears healed.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the player's 'U8P_WOUNDTOR::look' / examine action on the item/NPC instance.

function_map.name: U8P_WOUNDTOR::look
function_map.role: Player-facing inspect handler that reads Item::getFrame and shows a short bark describing wounded vs healed state.

function_map.name: U8P_WOUNDTOR::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Proximity/trigger entry process that starts or advances the object's timed toggling by calling U8P_WOUNDTOR::func013A.

function_map.name: U8P_WOUNDTOR::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Proximity/trigger exit process that unregisters scheduled callbacks/refs (cleanup) using FREE::resetRef.

function_map.name: U8P_WOUNDTOR::gotHit
function_map.role: Damage/hit handler that plays a hit sound effect for feedback; declared with parameters (uword, word).

function_map.name: U8P_WOUNDTOR::func013A
function_map.role: Internal toggle/scheduler: reads frame, flips between frames 0 and 1 (plays SFX on certain transitions), computes a random delay and spawns a deferred follow-up (scheduling behavior).

internal_calls.purpose: Start or advance the object's toggle/timed behavior when an actor enters proximity.

external_calls.purpose: Read the current visual/animation frame to decide whether to display 'Wounded Torax' or 'Healed Torax'.
Inspect current frame to choose toggle behavior.
Set the new visual frame (toggle between 0 and 1).
Play a sound when flipping from frame 1 to 0 (audible feedback for the transition).
Play a hit sound effect when the object is damaged.
Unregister or clear the scheduled reference or callback tied to this object to stop periodic behavior.
Compute a randomized delay used to schedule a deferred call.
Schedule a deferred call to FREE::func1C19 (follow-up/cleanup) using the computed delay.
Target of the spawned delayed call; likely continues or clears the scheduled behavior after the delay (inferred).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script drives a simple 'Torax' item/NPC that visually alternates between "wounded" and "healed" frames, reports its current visible state when examined by the player, responds to hits with a sound, and schedules periodic/randomized toggles while a player is nearby.
